Joshua,
Some questions re qualifying to be a host for the Film Festival came up at our Green Team meeting at Trinity Lutheran, Des Plaines. 
How exactly is the $600 requirement handled? Is the money sent to One Earth ahead of time? If the money isn’t completely spent, do we get that back? Or do we maintain an account and pay for bills your organization identifies? Am I missing something else re these funds?
If the tech support we describe in our application is not acceptable, does your organization provide someone, do we pay that person or do we find someone who qualifies per One Earth standards? 
Can we be guaranteed a date maybe two months ahead so as not to conflict with our church calendar? 
We have already reached out a bit to assess if there is any interest from our friends in co-sponsoring this in the event we would be accepted. Would One Earth also assist in finding a co-sponsor?
That’s all of my meeting notes at the moment. Thanks for helping us understand better what we need to consider in taking on this very important responsibility,
John Stoffregen

Hi Ms. Casey, Good Morning, My name is Sara London. I am a second-year law student at Loyola University Chicago and an executive board member for the LUC Environmental Law Society.  I am reaching out because the LUC Environmental Law Society is interested in hosting a documentary screening next Spring for earth week and am wondering if One Earth Film Fest would be interesting in partnering to host a screening at the law school in 2026.  Please let me know what additional information I may provide.  Thank you in advance for your consideration!  Best regards,
Sara London
Village of Oak Park at Oak Park Village Hall (with Sustainability Department, Chief Sustainability Officer)

Hi Ana; I wanted to let you know that the Village would be interested in hosting a film again this year. I do have a question. Our Board Chambers is not the best venue. Do you have any contacts at Lake Street Theater by any chance? Has the film festival ever partnered with them to show a film? (Ana said yes but it costs $500; Lindsey said they could maybe swing that but want to discuss a potential 3rd location to share between VOP + OPPL). Thank you!
